--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Our dashboard took three full seconds to become usable, and I had convinced myself that was just what a data-heavy Angular app cost.
Then a customer sent a screen recording. Three seconds of a blank screen, followed by a visible jump as everything popped in at once. Watching it from their side, not mine, was the moment I stopped making excuses for the framework and started actually measuring my own code.
Eight fixes later, that same screen loaded in three hundred milliseconds. Not a rewrite. Not a framework change. The same app, the same data, eight specific decisions made differently.
I tracked the time to interactive after every single fix, so what follows is not a theory, it is the actual number, dropping, one honest change at a time.
Where We Started
Time to interactive on a mid-range laptop, throttled to a typical 4G connection, on our main dashboard screen: 3000 ms.
That is the number every fix below is measured against.
Fix 1: Lazy Loading Every Feature Route Instead of Bundling Everything Upfront
The problem
